acceptance: 1001st write in window returns 429 with Retry-After. After window expires writes succeed. Reads never rate-limited. rate_limit.enabled=false disables. Config values respected. Tests: exceed limit, wait for window reset, verify reads unaffected.
description: Sliding window rate limiter for entity writes keyed by actor, in axon-server. Default: 1000 mutations/60s per actor. On breach: 429 with Retry-After header and {code: RATE_LIMITED, actor, limit, window_seconds, retry_after_seconds}. In-memory, per-process. Reads not rate-limited. Configurable via server config: rate_limit.writes_per_window, rate_limit.window_seconds, rate_limit.enabled.

ADR-005 and FEAT-012 define  as an explicit development escape hatch; leaving it on by default means the shipped server remains unauthenticated unless operators know to force auth on, which defeats the purpose of this implementation bead and leaves production deployments exposed to anonymous admin access.

acceptance: An upgraded Postgres database containing a legacy schema row stored under the raw qualified collection string is fully cleaned up by unregister_collection; a regression test seeds that legacy row and verifies no schema metadata remains after qualified unregister.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:215
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:1180
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:2166
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: ensure_namespace_catalog_tables backfills only database_name and schema_name for existing schema rows by joining on s.collection = c.name, but it never rewrites legacy schema rows whose collection column still contains the raw qualified identifier. The reviewed change makes unregister_collection delete schema rows only under the normalized bare key (key.collection). On an upgraded database that still has pre-fix schema metadata keyed as prod.billing.invoices, calling unregister_collection(prod.billing.invoices) deletes the normalized collection row but leaves those legacy schema-history rows orphaned. The new regression test covers only normalized rows, so this upgrade path remains unverified.
Suggested fix: Extend the upgrade or unregister cleanup path to remove legacy schema rows keyed by the raw qualified collection string in addition to the normalized bare key, and add a regression test that seeds a pre-fix schema row before running qualified unregister on an upgraded adapter.
title: correctness: upgraded Postgres schema metadata can survive qualified unregister

Description: Commit 6d5d274c853369e2e61c580fcf3854edfa5ea929 is the first commit that appends a closed hx-7bba596b row to .ddx/beads.jsonl, but that new row records both closing_commit_sha and the top-level <report-summary><commit> as 7bf664aba4653449dada51accf2db6c8974c4edf. As a result, tracker queries against the latest closed hx-7bba596b record misattribute which commit actually measured and closed the governing bead, breaking the durable traceability required by HELIX report semantics.\nSuggested fix: Append a new closed hx-7bba596b row that preserves the existing PASS measure-results block but updates both closing_commit_sha and the top-level <report-summary><commit> to 6d5d274c853369e2e61c580fcf3854edfa5ea929, with summary text that names that same close step.

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:354
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The same closing row for hx-9bf2b57c records a PASS <measure-results> block whose <gates> section includes only tracker commands (ddx bead show and scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py). In this repository, the active rust-cargo concern applies to tracker-only work as durable gate accounting: earlier review findings such as hx-c6da8e7e already treated omitted cargo deny/machete evidence as governance drift, and recent tracker/doc-only reviews either recorded rust-cargo gates as pass or explicitly marked them partial/not-run with scope-specific detail. Here, commit e0b5ca3da889b2ee6f13e45c46ef20021e56da54 even claims cargo check, cargo fmt --check, cargo test, and cargo clippy -- -D warnings in its Verification section, but none of that gate surface is reflected in hx-9bf2b57c's durable notes, and cargo deny check / cargo machete are neither recorded nor explained as not applicable. That leaves the bead's PASS measurement under-specified relative to the active concern.
Suggested fix: Refresh hx-9bf2b57c's latest <measure-results> so it records the rust-cargo quality gates relevant to this repo, including cargo check, cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check, cargo deny check, and cargo machete crates, or explicitly mark any skipped gates as partial/not applicable with scope-specific evidence for this tracker-only change.

acceptance: A scoped validation fixture ignores <measure-results> snippets that appear outside actual top-level measurement blocks while still validating the real block; regression tests cover fake-before-real and fake-after-real notes; malformed or duplicate real measurement blocks still fail.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py:15
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new fix parses timestamps structurally only after MEASURE_RESULTS_RE has already scanned the entire notes string for <measure-results>...</measure-results> substrings. That means any quoted or narrative <measure-results> snippet that appears outside an actual measurement block is still treated as real evidence. Reproduction: run the validator against a one-record fixture whose notes are <report-summary>quoted prior <measure-results><timestamp>2026-04-09T18:53:00Z</timestamp></measure-results></report-summary><measure-results><timestamp>2026-04-09T18:59:11Z</timestamp></measure-results> with --id hx-fake-outside-real; the command exits 0 and iter_measure_timestamps() returns two valid timestamps even though only the second block is a real measurement record. This under-validates scoped review evidence because report-summary prose or other note text can synthesize fake measurement blocks.
Suggested fix: Replace regex block extraction with structural parsing that enumerates only actual top-level <measure-results> elements in notes, then keep the direct-child timestamp parsing within those blocks. Add regression tests for fake-before-real and fake-after-real summary snippets so only genuine measurement blocks count.
title: correctness: tracker validator counts quoted <measure-results> outside measurement blocks

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:687
Category: drift
Severity: high
Description: Commit ca3b3544f53ccff507211c271fdc1d8d24fd30c1 has the same tree as its parent bb729bfe06aeedad147eeff3f78b343ac591c5e0, so the supposed tracker-close step for hx-7607eb91 never became durable. Evidence: `git rev-parse HEAD^{tree}` and `git rev-parse HEAD~1^{tree}` both return 5ea47d2fb844deb436f995297568e4e53c1a08f0, `git diff HEAD~1 HEAD` is empty, and the committed `.ddx/beads.jsonl` blob still ends at line 687 with hx-02953298 and contains no hx-7607eb91 row. The commit message claims verification with `ddx bead show hx-7607eb91 --json`, but that command fails against the committed tracker data because the bead was never persisted. This breaks HELIX durability: the last commit reports a review-finding closure that later tracker queries cannot recover at all.
Suggested fix: Re-run the tracker close step with a non-empty commit that actually appends the hx-7607eb91 record to `.ddx/beads.jsonl` and records its measure/report closure evidence in committed state, so `ddx bead show hx-7607eb91 --json` succeeds on the committed tree.

Description: The implementation now emits template.create, template.update, and template.delete audit entries, and FEAT-026 depends on those public operation names for template lifecycle provenance. FEAT-003 still declares the audit operation field as one of only entity.create, entity.update, entity.delete, collection.create, collection.drop, and schema.update. That leaves the core audit specification out of sync with the shipped API surface and can mislead future API, CLI, or UI work that treats the enumerated list as exhaustive.
Suggested fix: Update FEAT-003 to include the template mutation operations in the operation taxonomy, or restate the field as an extensible operation namespace with FEAT-specific additions cross-referenced from FEAT-026.

acceptance: A regression test covers a shape-compatible helper contract that uses non-EntityRecord type names, and fetchEntity()/fetchEntities() accept the contract when the required fields and arguments are present; the targeted probe still avoids the full __schema.types scan.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: ui/src/lib/graphql.ts:90
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The narrowed helper-contract probe now assumes the backend exposes the helper object types under the exact names EntityRecord, EntityConnection, and EntityEdge. That assumption is not part of the helper query contract: GraphQL selection sets depend on field shape, and FEAT-015 / ADR-012 only standardize CollectionMeta, EntityConnection, EntityEdge, and PageInfo for the generic schema, not an EntityRecord type. The old implementation resolved the actual return type name from __schema.types, but the new switch in getNamedType() only returns metadata for the hard-coded aliases. Reproduced by stubbing /graphql so entity(collection, id) returns a shape-compatible TaskRecord and entities(...) returns TaskConnection; fetchEntity() now rejects with "Axon /graphql does not expose the entity helper contract yet" after a single probe call even though the queried fields are present.
Suggested fix: Keep the targeted probe, but resolve named types generically from the probed field metadata instead of keying on hard-coded aliases. For example, fetch the specific type names discovered on the root fields (or preserve a small name-to-metadata map) so helper support is validated by field shape rather than exact type names.
title: correctness: GraphQL helper probe hard-codes helper type names

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:182
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The refreshed hx-1012e833 tracker record writes <measure-results><timestamp>2026-04-09T22:01:00Z</timestamp>, but the same bead record has updated_at=2026-04-09T17:31:57.867644231Z and the reviewing commit 9284f4f7c96697bbd0735de7b6c745feed6fee91 was committed at 2026-04-09T17:39:00Z. That means the measurement evidence is timestamped more than four hours after both the recorded bead update and the commit that introduced it. HELIX review notes are supposed to be durable, queryable audit evidence; a future-dated measurement block makes the record internally inconsistent and weakens traceability for when the review actually happened.
Suggested fix: Regenerate the hx-1012e833 measurement note with a timestamp derived from the actual write time in UTC and ensure tracker-written measure timestamps do not exceed the enclosing bead's updated_at/commit time for the same change. Add a tracker regression or write-path assertion if this timestamp skew can be produced automatically.

acceptance: With collections named tasks created in both default and prod, GET /db/prod/collections returns only the prod collection metadata, GET /collections still returns the unscoped result set, and gRPC ListCollections with x-axon-database=prod matches the prod-scoped HTTP behavior under automated regression tests.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs:880
File: crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs:1331
File: crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:697
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: The new current-database transport work nests data_routes() under /db/{database} and advertises gRPC metadata scoping, but the shared list_collections handlers still ignore the selected database entirely. GET /db/prod/collections calls the same global AxonHandler::list_collections(ListCollectionsRequest {}) path as GET /collections, and AxonServiceImpl::list_collections likewise ignores x-axon-database. I verified this live against the reviewed commit by creating tasks in both default and prod, then calling GET /db/prod/collections; the response returned both rows instead of only the prod collection. That violates FEAT-014's database-isolation acceptance (listing collections in database teamA returns zero results for teamB's collections) and makes the new scoped transport surface unusable for tenants.
Suggested fix: Thread the selected current database into collection-listing requests on both HTTP and gRPC, filter the results to that database before responding, and add regression coverage that seeds same-named collections in default and prod and asserts the scoped HTTP and gRPC listings only return the selected database's collections.
title: integration: current-database collection listings still return global results

description: FEAT-001 and FEAT-005 specify CLI commands for collection describe, collection drop, schema show, and schema validate. Currently only collection create and collection list exist.
title: Add missing CLI subcommands: collection describe/drop, schema show/validate

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:411
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The reviewed commit closes review-finding hx-f058cf32 by appending a closed tracker record, but it does not add any notes or close-summary evidence showing what actually satisfied the bead's acceptance criteria. ID:       hx-f058cf32
Title:    drift: rust-cargo dependency added outside workspace manifest
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 0
Labels:   helix, phase:build, review-finding, area:api
Owner:    erik
Desc:     Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml:49
Category: drift (rust-cargo)
Severity: medium
Description: The reviewed commit adds reqwest directly under crates/axon-server dev-dependencies instead of threading the new third-party crate through [workspace.dependencies] and consuming it with reqwest.workspace = true. The active rust-cargo concern explicitly requires new dependencies to be added to the workspace manifest first so versioning, audits, and future upgrades stay centralized. This patch followed that rule for uuid but not for reqwest, so the dependency addition is inconsistent with the repository's declared Rust practices.
Suggested fix: Add reqwest to [workspace.dependencies] in the workspace Cargo.toml, switch crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml to reqwest.workspace = true while keeping the needed stream feature in the workspace declaration, and re-run the dependency hygiene gates.
Accept:   Workspace Cargo.toml declares reqwest under [workspace.dependencies], crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml references it via reqwest.workspace = true, and cargo deny check plus cargo machete crates both pass.
Created:  2026-04-09 17:17:20
Updated:  2026-04-09 17:20:18
Claimed:  2026-04-09T17:19:04Z
Machine:  sindri
spec-id: crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml now reports only the original description and ; it does not record that commit c655f51 centralized the reqwest dependency or that advisories ok, bans ok, licenses ok, sources ok and cargo-machete didn't find any unused dependencies in crates. Good job! passed. HELIX's measure/report workflow requires durable, queryable verification evidence on the governing bead. Without that note, later review or audit cannot tell why this finding was considered resolved, even though the underlying code fix is present.
Suggested fix: Before or alongside closing a review-finding bead, write durable notes summarizing the fix commit and the acceptance verification results, then keep the close record. For hx-f058cf32 specifically, record that c655f51 moved reqwest into [workspace.dependencies] and that advisories ok, bans ok, licenses ok, sources ok plus cargo-machete didn't find any unused dependencies in crates. Good job! passed.

acceptance: The workspace Cargo.toml declares tokio-stream under [workspace.dependencies], and crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml references tokio-stream.workspace = true everywhere it is used.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml:31
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: This commit adds tokio-stream directly in crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml instead of declaring it under [workspace.dependencies] and consuming it with workspace = true. The active rust-cargo concern requires new dependencies to be workspace-managed so versioning and audits stay centralized.
Suggested fix: Add tokio-stream to [workspace.dependencies] in the workspace Cargo.toml and switch both dependency entries in crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml to tokio-stream.workspace = true.

acceptance: Force-dropping a namespace or database removes entity rows only for collections inside the dropped scope, same-named collections in surviving namespaces/databases retain their entities, and regression tests cover Memory, SQLite, and Postgres shared-name scenarios.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs:201
File: crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs:790
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:580
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: The new purge paths delete by bare collection name, but all three storage adapters still key entity storage by bare collection name as well. FEAT-014 explicitly allows the same collection name to exist in multiple namespaces and databases. As a result, force-dropping prod.billing.invoices can also delete surviving data in prod.engineering.invoices or default.default.invoices. The existing same-name tests only check catalog membership, so this regression is currently untested.
Suggested fix: Scope entity storage and purge operations by fully qualified collection identity (database/schema + collection, or collection numeric ID), and add regression tests proving force-drop preserves same-named collections in surviving namespaces/databases across Memory, SQLite, and Postgres.
title: integration: force-drop purges same-named collections outside the target namespace/database

acceptance: grep -c 'same transaction' ADR-003 returns 0 for lines describing V1 audit write behavior (references to durable backend same-transaction goal are acceptable); diagram shows begin_tx() before version check; risk mitigation references post-commit strategy and recovery invariant
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-003-backing-store-architecture.md:67,169,252,267
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit f3262b1 updated the audit write path section of ADR-003 to describe the post-commit audit strategy, but four other sections in the same document still claim audit writes happen in the same storage transaction as entity writes:
  (1) Line 67: Atomicity bullet still says audit is in the same transaction
  (2) Line 169: StorageAdapter key design points says entity and audit writes share a transaction handle
  (3) Line 252: Consequences says audit overhead is from append in same transaction
  (4) Line 267: Risk mitigation says audit and entity writes share a transaction
Additionally, the audit write path diagram (lines 72-89) shows StorageAdapter.begin_tx() after version check and entity writes, but code calls begin_tx() first (transaction.rs:149).
Suggested fix: Update all four locations to reference the post-commit strategy. Fix the diagram to show begin_tx() before version check. Update the risk mitigation to reference the recovery invariant instead of shared-transaction atomicity.
title: correctness: ADR-003 has residual same-transaction audit claims after post-commit update

acceptance: Calling unregister_collection with a qualified collection ID removes the Postgres collections, schemas, and collection_views rows stored under the resolved bare collection key for that namespace; a regression test covers qualified unregister after schema and collection-view writes.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:1168
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:1180
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:1192
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: This commit normalizes Postgres schema and collection-view rows to the resolved bare collection key (key.collection), but unregister_collection still deletes collection_views, schemas, and collections using the caller's raw collection string. A qualified unregister such as unregister_collection(prod.billing.invoices) resolves the namespace correctly, then issues WHERE collection = 'prod.billing.invoices' even though the persisted rows now use invoices. The delete statements become no-ops, leaving the registry row and metadata behind.
Suggested fix: Use the resolved catalog key consistently in unregister_collection (key.collection for collection/name) and add a regression test that unregistering a uniquely resolvable qualified collection removes the collection row plus associated schema/view metadata.
title: correctness: unregister_collection still targets raw qualified metadata keys

acceptance: Dropping a collection removes any stored collection view in every storage adapter. Recreating the same collection starts with no collection view and versioning from 1. A regression test covers drop+recreate behavior.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:1160
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: drop_collection deletes entities, schema, and the collection registry entry, but never calls delete_collection_view. The new collection_views stores introduced in this commit therefore retain templates after a collection is dropped. If the same collection name is recreated later, the stale template and incremented view version silently reappear, which violates collection lifecycle expectations and leaves orphaned presentation state behind.
Suggested fix: Delete the collection view as part of the drop path and add an integration test that creates a view, drops the collection, recreates it, and verifies no prior view remains.
title: integration: dropping a collection leaves its collection view behind

File: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-markdown-templates.md:322
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: The new public template PUT/GET/DELETE surfaces persist and delete collection views directly, but neither put_collection_template nor delete_collection_template appends an audit entry, and the audit mutation enum has no template-specific operation at all. FEAT-026 explicitly requires template changes to be audited with actor, timestamp, and before/after state. As shipped, operators can change or remove a collection's presentation layer without any durable audit trail, violating both the feature contract and Axon's audit-first architecture.
Suggested fix: Introduce audit mutation types for template create/update/delete (or an equivalent collection-view mutation), capture before/after template state, and append audit entries from the handler on successful template writes and deletes. Add handler and gateway tests that query the audit log and verify actor plus before/after metadata for template changes.","id":"hx-9ede52ae","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs","status":"closed","title":"correctness: template CRUD writes are not audited","updated_at":"2026-04-09T09:34:49.439736591Z"}

acceptance: MCP resource and prompt handlers map AxonError::NotFound to McpError::NotFound, validation-style failures to McpError::InvalidParams, and storage/internal failures to McpError::Internal; regression tests cover at least one non-not-found failure path.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-mcp/src/resources.rs:402
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new read-side adapters convert multiple AxonHandler failures directly into McpError::NotFound (describe_collection, get_entity, and list_neighbors in resources; the same pattern also appears in crates/axon-mcp/src/prompts.rs:244). Because AxonError also carries storage, validation, and invalid-operation failures, backend faults or bad input can now be misreported to MCP clients as missing resources. That breaks structured agent handling and hides real incidents behind false not-found responses.
Suggested fix: Add a shared AxonError-to-McpError mapper that preserves not-found vs validation vs internal failures, and use it in resources and prompts instead of hard-coding McpError::NotFound.

As a result, tracker queries against the latest closed hx-9b720e48 record misattribute which commit actually measured and closed the governing bead, breaking the durable traceability required by HELIX report semantics.
Suggested fix: Append a new closed hx-9b720e48 row that preserves the existing PARTIAL measure-results block but updates both closing_commit_sha and the top-level <report-summary><commit> to the commit that actually appends the corrective closure row, with summary text that names that same close step.

Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The last commit adds markdown regression tests that use `.unwrap()` on handler, storage, and gateway setup calls in the touched files. The active `rust-cargo` concern requires the strict clippy gate `cargo clippy --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings -D clippy::todo -D clippy::unimplemented -D clippy::dbg_macro -D clippy::print_stdout -D clippy::unwrap_used`, and the new lines are included in that failure set. That means this review-fix commit improves coverage but still lands new code that violates the declared no-`unwrap` quality bar for Rust targets.
Suggested fix: Replace the new `.unwrap()` calls in the added markdown tests with `expect()` or `expect_err()` carrying specific failure messages, or restructure the assertions so the tests remain explicit without using unwrap-family calls. Re-run the strict clippy command and confirm the touched markdown tests no longer contribute `unwrap_used` violations.
title: drift: markdown regression tests introduce unwrap_used violations

acceptance: When default and prod each contain audit history, GET /db/prod/audit/query and GET /audit/query with X-Axon-Database=prod return only prod audit entries, while an unscoped audit query still returns both databases' entries in regression tests.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs:748
File: crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs:1331
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:1177
Category: security
Severity: high
Description: The reviewed change qualifies the optional collection query parameter for /audit/query, but it never scopes the audit query itself to the selected current database. Because the router now exposes /db/{database}/audit/query and header-based current-database selection, a tenant-scoped request still returns every audit entry when collection is omitted. I verified this live against the reviewed commit by creating tasks in both default and prod and then calling both GET /db/prod/audit/query and GET /audit/query with X-Axon-Database: prod; both responses included the default-database collection.create entry. FEAT-014 requires database isolation and explicitly states audit log entries for teamA are not visible from teamB, so the new transport surface currently leaks cross-database provenance.
Suggested fix: Add database-level filtering to the audit query path when a current database is selected, preserving fully qualified explicit collection filters while excluding entries outside the selected database, and add regression tests covering both the /db/{database} path prefix and the X-Axon-Database header form.
title: security: current-database audit queries leak entries from other databases

description:

File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:401
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit f6bdfd824c07ce0db2c1c77bd82d77a28c74867e closes review-finding hx-6064816c by changing only its status from in_progress to closed. The bead already contains a <measure-results> block, but unlike other closed review beads in this tracker it does not append any durable <report-summary> or equivalent close summary for the closure itself. As a result, `ddx bead show hx-6064816c` exposes only the original finding text and `Status: closed`, with no queryable summary of what was done, the reused verification basis mentioned in the commit message, or the final measure/report disposition. HELIX's report workflow requires the governing bead closure to summarize what was done, the measurement outcome, follow-on creation, and relevant references. Without that closure summary, later tracker queries cannot distinguish an intentional, measured close from a bare status flip.
Suggested fix: Update hx-6064816c with a durable close/report summary before or alongside closing it. The summary should name hx-6064816c as the governing bead, record commit f6bdfd824c07ce0db2c1c77bd82d77a28c74867e, state that the prior measure evidence on hx-f058cf32 was reused and rechecked via `ddx bead show hx-6064816c` and `scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py .ddx/beads.jsonl --id hx-6064816c`, and preserve the closed status.
Close summary: Added durable closure text to governing bead hx-6064816c so its record now names issue hx-6064816c and commit f6bdfd824c07ce0db2c1c77bd82d77a28c74867e, summarizes the reused verification basis, preserves the existing PASS measure-results block, and leaves the bead closed.
Verification: `ddx bead show hx-6064816c`, `ddx bead show hx-6064816c --json`, and `scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py .ddx/beads.jsonl --id hx-6064816c` all passed after the tracker update.

acceptance: A gateway test injects a render-failing template and asserts GET /collections/{collection}/entities/{id}?format=markdown returns 500 with both error detail and the entity JSON payload; cargo test -p axon-server passes
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:278-302
File: crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs:286-307
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new markdown GET path renders through `AxonHandler::get_entity_markdown`, which returns only a `String`. When rendering fails, the handler maps the error into `AxonError::Storage`, and the gateway converts that into a generic 500 JSON error with no entity payload. FEAT-026 explicitly requires that if `format=markdown` rendering fails, the response still includes the entity JSON so callers retain access to the underlying data. As shipped, any invalid persisted template or other render-time failure drops the entity body entirely.
Suggested fix: Change the markdown GET flow to preserve the fetched entity on render failure and serialize it in the 500 response body alongside the error detail. Add an integration test that injects a bad stored template and asserts the 500 body includes both the error and the entity payload.
title: correctness: markdown GET 500 responses omit entity payload on render failure

acceptance: Dry-run rollback of a deleted entity returns a non-error preview without writing state, and handler plus HTTP regression tests cover the deleted-entity dry-run flow
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:1426
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new deleted-entity rollback path restores the entity on an applied rollback, but it still hard-fails dry_run requests with InvalidOperation("dry-run rollback preview is unavailable for deleted entities"). FEAT-023 says all rollback operations support a dry_run flag, and the governing rollback bead records the HTTP contract as dry_run=true returns a non-writing preview. This commit codifies the deleted-entity case as an exception instead of extending the preview surface to cover the recovery flow it just added, so agents cannot safely inspect a restore-after-delete before applying it.
Suggested fix: Extend the rollback dry-run response to represent deleted current state and return a preview for restore-after-delete instead of an error, then add handler and HTTP regression tests for deleted-entity dry_run.
title: correctness: deleted-entity rollback still rejects dry-run preview

acceptance: handle_put_schema creates an audit entry with MutationType::SchemaUpdate; PutSchemaRequest includes actor field; test exists asserting audit entry is written after put_schema
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:519-525
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: handle_put_schema stores a schema without creating an audit entry. MutationType::SchemaUpdate exists in the audit system but is never used. Additionally PutSchemaRequest lacks an actor field, so audit provenance cannot be recorded. Violates Principle P2 (100% mutation coverage: no code path mutates state without audit entry).
Suggested fix: Add actor: Option<String> to PutSchemaRequest, emit an AuditEntry with MutationType::SchemaUpdate in handle_put_schema before returning.
title: correctness: handle_put_schema missing audit entry

acceptance: After rendering markdown through a bare collection alias, creating a same-named sibling collection, and dropping one namespace or database, any cached template associated with the dropped collection is evicted even if the bare alias is ambiguous at drop time; rendering the surviving collection via the bare alias uses the surviving collection's template; regression tests cover both namespace and database drop paths.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:142
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:1605
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:1663
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `invalidate_markdown_templates_for_collections()` only evicts cache entries whose current `resolve_collection_key()` call succeeds. That misses entries cached under a bare collection alias when the alias becomes temporarily ambiguous before the drop. Concretely: render `notes` while only `prod.billing.notes` exists, create `prod.engineering.notes`, then drop `prod.billing`. During invalidation `resolve_collection_key("notes")` errors with "exists in multiple namespaces", so the bare `notes` cache entry is retained. After the drop, `notes` resolves to the surviving collection again and `compiled_markdown_template()` can reuse the dropped namespace's compiled template if the view version matches, returning incorrect markdown for the surviving collection. The new tests only exercise fully qualified collection IDs, so this alias path regresses silently.
Suggested fix: Evict cache entries by comparing normalized qualified keys without depending on current alias resolution success, or explicitly remove any cached alias that previously referred to a doomed collection. Add regression tests that render through a bare alias, introduce a same-named sibling collection, drop one namespace/database, and verify subsequent bare-alias markdown renders the surviving collection's template.
title: correctness: markdown cache eviction misses ambiguous bare aliases

Planning evidence:
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-admin-web-ui.md:22-29 and 42-98 require a browser UI for collections, entities, schemas, audit, and a /health-backed indicator, served as static files by axon-server.

Implementation evidence:
- ui/package.json:5-15 omits Bun-specific scripts, typecheck, lint, and test gates, and still targets Svelte 4.
- ui/src/routes/+page.svelte:10-20 uses placeholder data instead of live API calls.
- ui/src/routes/schemas/+page.svelte:4-27 and ui/src/routes/audit/+page.svelte:4-12 keep only local state with no HTTP integration.
- ui/src/routes/+layout.svelte:5-13 provides only a static nav and no health indicator, while crates/axon-server/src/main.rs:16-34 exposes no --ui-dir serving path.

Gap:
The repo contains a scaffold UI, but the FEAT-011 shipped baseline is not implemented.

acceptance: PUT /collections/{name}/schema accepts an optional actor field in the request body; gateway integration test asserts audit entry actor matches the provided value; existing put_schema tests continue to pass
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs:505-522
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The put_schema gateway endpoint accepts Json<CollectionSchema> directly (line 508) and hardcodes actor: None when constructing PutSchemaRequest (line 518). Every other mutating endpoint uses a custom body struct with actor: Option<String> (CreateEntityBody, UpdateEntityBody, DeleteEntityBody, etc.) and passes body.actor through. This means REST API callers cannot provide audit provenance for schema updates — the audit actor will always be 'anonymous'. The handler layer supports actor (added in hx-2a25bfc6) but the HTTP layer does not pass it through.
Suggested fix: Create a PutSchemaBody wrapper struct (containing schema fields plus actor: Option<String>), use it as the Json extractor type in put_schema, and pass body.actor into PutSchemaRequest. Add a gateway integration test verifying actor is recorded in the audit entry after PUT /collections/{name}/schema.
title: integration: gateway put_schema endpoint does not pass actor to handler

acceptance: A scoped validation run against a fixture JSONL where the requested bead exists but has no notes or no <measure-results> exits non-zero and names that bead as unvalidated, while scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py .ddx/beads.jsonl --id hx-f058cf32 --id hx-1012e833 still exits 0 after validating those measured beads.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py:40
File: scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py:84
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The commit fixes the missing-bead-ID path, but scoped validation can still report a clean pass when the requested bead exists yet has no notes or no <measure-results> payload. In validate_tracker(), a matched bead is counted as present before the `if not notes or not updated_at: continue` skip, and the CLI then prints success at line 84 when failures stays empty. Reproduced against a one-line JSONL fixture containing {"id":"hx-missing-notes","updated_at":"2026-04-09T18:00:00Z"}: scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py /tmp/axon_tracker_sample.jsonl --id hx-missing-notes exits 0 and prints a success message even though no measurement evidence was validated. Because this validator is used to prove durable tracker evidence for specific beads, that remaining vacuous-success path can still let scoped verification silently validate nothing.
Suggested fix: In scoped mode, fail closed when a requested bead lacks notes, lacks <measure-results>, or otherwise yields zero validated timestamps. Report the affected bead IDs explicitly, and keep the current success path only for requested beads that both exist and contribute actual measurement evidence, or intentionally document a different contract and rename the command/output accordingly.

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:400
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit ede07bbca7e9457336414169bd1853a8d572822c refreshes review-finding hx-b08b255d so hx-84991cc7 now records the missing closing commit, but the governing bead hx-b08b255d is itself closed with a durable <report-summary> that records only <artifact>.ddx/beads.jsonl:hx-84991cc7</artifact> and never names the commit that actually measured and closed hx-b08b255d. HELIX report guidance for this repository’s tracker-governance chain has already ratcheted on preserving the closing commit for repaired beads, so later tracker queries should be able to recover which execution commit closed the governing bead. As committed, ddx bead show hx-b08b255d --json can confirm the bead is closed and which artifact was refreshed, but it cannot recover that commit ede07bbca7e9457336414169bd1853a8d572822c performed the hx-b08b255d closure.
Suggested fix: Append a new closed hx-b08b255d record that preserves the current PASS measure-results and artifact reference to .ddx/beads.jsonl:hx-84991cc7 while adding a machine-readable <report-summary><commit>ede07bbca7e9457336414169bd1853a8d572822c</commit> for the execution that closed hx-b08b255d.

acceptance: AxonHandler constructed with a pre-populated StorageAdapter (containing entities and schemas) correctly reports those collections via list_collections and describe_collection; test: create collection, drop and reconstruct AxonHandler, verify list_collections returns the collection
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:38
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: collections: HashSet<CollectionId> is an in-memory data structure not persisted via StorageAdapter. When using a durable backend (SQLite), entities and schemas survive restart but the collection registry does not. After restart, list_collections returns empty, describe_collection returns NotFound, and drop_collection returns NotFound — even though entities and schemas still exist in storage. FEAT-001 NFR states: Durability: Collection metadata is durable — survives process restart in both embedded and server modes.
Suggested fix: Add collection metadata persistence to the StorageAdapter trait (e.g. list_collections, register_collection, unregister_collection) and load the set on AxonHandler construction, or reconstruct from stored schemas at startup.
title: correctness: collection registry is ephemeral — not persisted via StorageAdapter

acceptance: Either: (1) Transaction::create/update/delete reject the 101st op with AxonError::InvalidArgument AND Transaction struct carries created_at with timeout enforcement in commit path; OR (2) ADR-004 Implementation Notes clearly mark these as not-yet-implemented with a reference to the implementing issue
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-004-transaction-model.md:103-110,210-211
File: crates/axon-api/src/transaction.rs (Transaction struct and commit method)
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: ADR-004 lines 103-110 state that the 101st stage_* call returns InvalidArgument and that the Transaction struct carries a creation timestamp for 30s timeout enforcement. Lines 210-211 say 'The 100-op limit and 30s timeout are checked in the commit path per FEAT-008.' However, transaction.rs has no op count check in create/update/delete methods, no created_at timestamp field on the Transaction struct, and no timeout check in commit/execute. The Implementation Notes read as descriptions of current state, not future work.
Suggested fix: Either (a) implement the 100-op limit check in stage methods and add a created_at field with timeout check in commit, or (b) move these items from Implementation Notes to a 'Not Yet Implemented' section to avoid misleading readers.
title: correctness: ADR-004 Implementation Notes claim 100-op limit and 30s timeout exist but they are not implemented

acceptance: A Postgres database created with the pre-fix collection_views schema removes any stored collection view when the collection is unregistered after upgrade. An automated regression test covers the Postgres upgrade path.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:43
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The Postgres fix likewise adds the `collection_views -> collections` foreign key only inside `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`. For databases that already have `collection_views` from the pre-fix schema, PostgreSQL leaves the existing table unchanged, so the new foreign key and `ON DELETE CASCADE` are never installed. `unregister_collection()` still deletes only from `collections`, which means upgraded Postgres deployments will retain stale collection-view rows after a collection is removed.
Suggested fix: Add an explicit Postgres migration or fallback cleanup path for upgraded databases. At minimum, `unregister_collection()` should delete from `collection_views` directly; ideally startup should ensure the foreign key exists on existing databases and an upgrade regression test should cover the path.
title: integration: Postgres upgrades keep stale collection views after unregister

acceptance: Dropping an /mcp/sse listener and reconnecting immediately with the same default session key does not inherit prior resources/subscribe state; an automated regression test covers the immediate-reconnect ordering without waiting for background cleanup.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-server/src/mcp_http.rs:121
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new disconnect cleanup runs in a detached task after connect() returns: it waits on sender.closed() and only then calls disconnect_listener(). That means a fast reconnect using the same default session key can reach get_or_create() before the cleanup task removes the old session. In that ordering, the reconnect reuses the old McpServer and inherits its prior subscriptions; when the cleanup task finally runs it only removes the old sender and leaves the reused session alive because the new listener is already present. The new regression test does not cover this because it explicitly waits until subscription_count() is None before reconnecting. FEAT-016 requires disconnect cleanup, and this race leaves the stale-subscription leak reachable for ordinary network-blip reconnects.
Suggested fix: Make session invalidation synchronous with stream teardown or add generation/session-token semantics so a reconnect cannot attach to a session that was already logically closed. Add a regression test that reconnects immediately after dropping the prior SSE receiver, without waiting for background cleanup, and proves no stale resource_updated notification is delivered.
title: integration: async MCP session cleanup races with immediate SSE reconnect

acceptance: ADR-004 Protocol section Commit sequence describes audit entries being flushed after commit_tx(), consistent with the Audit Integration section and the code in transaction.rs
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-004-transaction-model.md:48-56
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The Protocol section (lines 48-56) describes the commit path as: Apply all entity writes → Write all audit entries → Commit storage transaction. This claims audit entries are written BEFORE commit_tx(), directly contradicting the Audit Integration section (lines 92-113) updated in commit f3262b1, which correctly describes the post-commit audit strategy. A reader of the Protocol section gets the wrong mental model of when audit writes happen.
Suggested fix: Update the Protocol Commit sequence to: Apply all entity writes → commit_tx() → Flush buffered audit entries. Add a note that audit entries are written post-commit (reference the Audit Integration section).
title: correctness: ADR-004 Protocol section still claims pre-commit audit writes

acceptance: axon entity get with --render markdown emits valid JSON when --output json is selected, valid YAML when --output yaml is selected, default or table output remains readable markdown, and CLI tests cover both structured output modes.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-cli/src/main.rs:887
File: docs/helix/01-frame/technical-requirements.md:419
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The new axon entity get <collection> <id> --render markdown path prints raw markdown with println! regardless of the global --output flag. Direct reproduction: ./target/debug/axon --output json entity get tasks t-001 --render markdown exits successfully and prints # hello, which is not valid JSON. The CLI contract requires table, JSON, and YAML output modes, so the new markdown surface silently violates the existing machine-readable output behavior expected by scripts and agents.
Suggested fix: Make markdown rendering honor --output json and --output yaml with structured output, while preserving raw markdown for table or default output, and add CLI tests covering JSON and YAML markdown output.
title: drift: markdown CLI ignores JSON and YAML output modes

acceptance: Force-dropping a namespace or database removes all entity rows for every affected collection, records collection-drop audit entries for each removed collection, and regression tests cover Memory, SQLite, and Postgres force-drop paths.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:1454
File: crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs:358
File: crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs:783
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:572
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: This commit removed the `drop_collection` loop from both `drop_namespace` and `drop_database`, but the storage adapters still only delete catalog rows (`collections`, schemas/views, namespace metadata). None of the three adapters purge entity data in their namespace/database drop implementations, and the handler no longer emits per-collection `CollectionDrop` audit entries on forced drops. As a result, `force=true` now leaves orphaned entity rows behind and silently skips the audit trail for collection removal, which violates FEAT-014's drop semantics and Axon's audit-first principle.
Suggested fix: Restore forced namespace/database deletion to purge entities collection-by-collection (or make adapter-level namespace/database drops delete entity rows and emit equivalent audit records), then add regression tests covering force-drop behavior for Memory, SQLite, and Postgres.
title: integration: force-dropping a namespace or database leaves entity rows behind

acceptance: Memory, SQLite, and Postgres store entity rows under namespace-qualified collection identity; the same entity ID can exist independently in `prod.billing.invoices` and `prod.engineering.invoices`; read/write/query/delete/count/index operations are isolated per namespace; regression tests prove isolation across all three adapters and the handler/API path.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs:90
File: crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs:203
File: crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs:68
File: crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs:553
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:57
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:365
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:153
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: The catalog fix is namespace-aware, but the data plane is still keyed only by bare `CollectionId`. Memory keeps entity data in `HashMap<CollectionId, ...>`, SQLite and Postgres still use `entities(collection, id)` without database/schema columns, and handler entity CRUD/query paths accept only bare collection names. That means `prod.billing.invoices` and `prod.engineering.invoices` still cannot hold independent entity sets: reads, writes, counts, scans, deletes, indexes, and OCC all collapse onto the same bare `invoices` partition. FEAT-014 US-036 requires these collections to be distinct, so the current implementation remains incorrect beyond the catalog metadata.
Suggested fix: Thread namespace-qualified collection identity through entity CRUD and indexing end to end. Storage backends need namespace-qualified entity keys (or stable numeric IDs looked up from qualified collections), and the API surface needs a way to address non-default namespaces explicitly without falling back to bare-name ambiguity.
title: correctness: entity CRUD still collapses same-named collections across namespaces

acceptance: 1. Transaction::delete calls check_op_limit and returns Err when at MAX_OPS. 2. A test creates 100 delete ops then asserts the 101st delete returns InvalidArgument. 3. cargo test -p axon-api passes.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/transaction.rs:129
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Transaction::delete does not call check_op_limit() before pushing a WriteOp, unlike create (line 91) and update (line 113). The docstring at line 128 claims it returns Err(InvalidArgument) if the transaction already has MAX_OPS operations, but the implementation never enforces this. Delete operations bypass the MAX_OPS=100 safety guard, allowing transactions to exceed the limit.
Suggested fix: Add self.check_op_limit()?; as the first statement in the delete method body, before constructing the sentinel entity.
title: correctness: Transaction::delete missing check_op_limit guard

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:270
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit e42f5798d717cf7f5c88c3b16652708bbb0b4d2f corrects hx-8e3d5edb's measure results, but the updated durable closure record on bead hx-8e3d5edb now writes <report-summary><issue>hx-7f202857</issue>. HELIX report semantics close and summarize the governing bead itself, and nearby closed tracker findings in this same file record their own bead ID in report-summary.issue (for example hx-97daa0a6 records issue hx-97daa0a6). Pointing hx-8e3d5edb's closure summary at the follow-on fix bead instead of the bead being closed weakens durable traceability: later tooling or reviewers can no longer tell from the closed row alone which bead the report-summary actually closes, and the summary now duplicates hx-7f202857 rather than uniquely identifying hx-8e3d5edb's closure.
Suggested fix: Update hx-8e3d5edb's <report-summary> so <issue> names hx-8e3d5edb (the bead being closed), preserving the existing PARTIAL status and summary text, and keep the fix bead reference elsewhere if needed (for example in the summary sentence or an explicit <fixed-by> field).

acceptance: A scoped validation run against a fixture whose requested bead contains one malformed <measure-results> block without <timestamp> and one later valid block exits non-zero and reports the malformed evidence, while a scoped run against a bead whose measure blocks all contain valid timestamps still exits 0.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py:63
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new scoped fail-closed path still treats a requested bead as valid if any regex match yields a good timestamp, even when the same notes payload also contains a malformed <measure-results> block with no <timestamp>. The loop at scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py:63-96 only iterates regex matches for blocks that already contain <timestamp>, so malformed measure blocks are invisible once a later valid block exists. Reproduction: run the validator against a one-record fixture whose notes are '<measure-results><status>PASS</status></measure-results><measure-results><timestamp>2026-04-09T17:59:59Z</timestamp></measure-results>' with '--id hx-malformed-and-valid'; the command exits 0 and prints success even though part of the requested bead's measurement evidence is malformed. This is realistic tracker input: the current tracker already contains timestamp-less <measure-results> payloads such as bead hx-59895788, and the tracker also contains beads with multiple <measure-results> blocks. That means scoped validation can still silently under-validate a requested bead's durable evidence if an invalid block is paired with a later valid one.
Suggested fix: Parse notes as explicit <measure-results> blocks instead of scanning only timestamp-bearing regex matches, fail when any block is missing a timestamp or contains multiple/invalid timestamps, and add a regression test covering mixed malformed-plus-valid measure blocks.

FEAT-014 is only partially sketched in-process and is unavailable as a durable server or CLI capability.
title: FEAT-014: persist databases and namespaces across storage, server, and CLI

acceptance: Deleted-entity rollback with dry_run=true returns a preview payload instead of InvalidOperation, does not recreate the entity, and regression tests cover the deleted dry-run flow in axon-api and axon-server.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:1426
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new deleted-entity rollback path restores the entity when applied, but it hard-errors when the same rollback is requested with dry_run=true. FEAT-023 requires all rollback operations to support dry_run and to return the set of changes that would be applied without mutating state. Because rollback_entity now returns InvalidOperation whenever current is absent, deleted entities cannot be previewed even though they can now be restored, leaving the endpoint out of spec for one of the core recovery cases this commit introduced.
Suggested fix: Extend deleted-entity rollback to return a dry-run preview instead of InvalidOperation, representing the missing current state explicitly and adding handler + HTTP regression coverage that dry_run for a deleted entity returns a preview and leaves storage unchanged.

    <criterion name="axon-api and axon-server regressions cover deleted dry-run flow" status="pass" evidence="Focused cargo tests for axon-api and axon-server passed with the new deleted-entity dry-run coverage."/>

acceptance: `python3 scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py .ddx/beads.jsonl --id hx-89c1b207` exits 0, and a regression fixture with one real measurement `<timestamp>` plus quoted `<timestamp>` text inside evidence also exits 0 while malformed duplicate real timestamps still fail.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py:24
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new block-level parser still uses a raw TIMESTAMP_TAG_RE over the full <measure-results> payload, so it counts literal <timestamp>...</timestamp> text that appears inside evidence strings as if it were a real measurement timestamp. This produces false positives on realistic tracker data. Reproduction 1: `python3 scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py .ddx/beads.jsonl --id hx-89c1b207` exits 1 with `measure-results block with multiple timestamps`, but hx-89c1b207 only has one actual measurement timestamp; the second match comes from quoted prior evidence text in its `<pass ... evidence='...<measure-results><timestamp>2026-04-09T18:53:00Z</timestamp>...'>`. Reproduction 2: running the validator against a one-record fixture whose single <measure-results> block contains one real <timestamp> element plus quoted <timestamp> text inside an evidence attribute also exits non-zero. That means the fix for malformed mixed blocks now rejects valid tracker notes that document prior malformed evidence, which undermines scoped audit verification on existing review beads.
Suggested fix: Parse the <measure-results> content structurally enough to distinguish the top-level measurement timestamp element from literal XML-like text embedded in attributes or child text, and add a regression test covering a valid measure block whose evidence quotes a prior <timestamp> snippet without turning it into a multiple-timestamp failure.

acceptance: HTTP requests can select a current database via X-Axon-Database or an equivalent /db/{name} route surface, gRPC requests honor x-axon-database metadata, unqualified collection names resolve against the selected database while default behavior remains default, and transport tests cover default, explicit, and isolated-database cases.
description:
Discovered from review issue hx-be06f5a5 under governing bead hx-4f140a3e.

Planning evidence:
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-014-multi-tenancy.md:89-95 requires unqualified collection names to resolve against the connection's current database selected via HTTP header X-Axon-Database, gRPC metadata x-axon-database, or a /db/{name}/... path prefix.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-011-multi-tenancy-and-namespace-hierarchy.md:116-124 and 312-314 define current-database resolution and the transport surfaces that carry it.

Implementation evidence:
- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs:1003-1025 exposes collection/entity/transaction routes plus database management routes, but no /db/{name} prefixed API surface and no request-header handling for current database selection.
- crates/axon-core/src/id.rs:52-58 only resolves names against default rather than a request-scoped current database.
- Repository search found no implementation of X-Axon-Database or x-axon-database handling.

Gap:
FEAT-014 transport-level current-database selection is unimplemented, so unqualified names cannot target a non-default database through the shipped HTTP or gRPC surfaces.
title: FEAT-014: add current-database selection for HTTP and gRPC unqualified collection operations

acceptance: put_schema() with an invalid entity_schema (e.g. {"type": "bogus"}) returns AxonError::SchemaValidation; existing tests continue to pass
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:52-53,69-72
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The public put_schema() method (line 52) and the deprecated register_schema() method (line 69) on AxonHandler write schemas directly to storage without calling compile_entity_schema(). While handle_put_schema (the HTTP-facing handler) now validates, these helper methods allow callers to persist an invalid entity_schema that would cause confusing validation errors on subsequent entity writes. This is inconsistent with Principle P5 (Schema Earns Its Keep) which requires validation on every write path.
Suggested fix: Add compile_entity_schema() validation to put_schema(). For register_schema(), either add the same validation or remove the method if it is truly deprecated.
title: correctness: put_schema and register_schema bypass entity_schema validation

acceptance: A single version-monotonicity check function exists in axon-sim, imported by both audit_completeness and transaction_atomicity workloads; no duplicate implementations remain
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-sim/src/audit_completeness.rs:274 and crates/axon-sim/src/transaction_atomicity.rs:261
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: check_version_monotonicity (audit_completeness.rs) and check_entity_version_monotonicity (transaction_atomicity.rs) are identical logic with different names. This DRY violation means a bug fix or enhancement to one may not be applied to the other, risking divergence in INV-007 checking across workloads.
Suggested fix: Extract the shared version monotonicity checker into a common module (e.g., axon-sim/src/invariants.rs or a shared helper in lib.rs) and have both workloads import it.
title: correctness: deduplicate version monotonicity check in axon-sim workloads

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:24
File: crates/axon-api/src/response.rs:188
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:1290
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The last commit updated the entity-level rollback planning bead to use the shipped audit operation name `entity.revert`, but the same bead still says the response includes `source_audit_id` and that the rollback audit trail stores a `source_audit_id` trailer. The shipped revert surface does not use those names: `RevertEntityResponse` returns `{ entity, audit_entry }`, and the audit metadata key recorded by the handler is `reverted_from_entry_id`. Leaving the bead partially updated means the rollback planning artifact still disagrees with the implementation it claims to mirror, which can send future execution or acceptance checks toward the wrong response and metadata contract.
Suggested fix: Update the entity-level rollback planning bead so its response and audit-metadata naming matches the current revert implementation, or explicitly document an intentional public API rename and the translation between `audit_entry`/`reverted_from_entry_id` and any future HTTP field names.

acceptance: An immediate reconnect through the live /mcp/sse transport after the prior client disconnects does not inherit the previous connection's subscriptions, and a regression test covers that transport-level ordering instead of dropping the raw receiver directly.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-server/src/mcp_http.rs:86
File: crates/axon-server/src/mcp_http.rs:214
File: crates/axon-server/src/mcp_http.rs:517
File: crates/axon-server/src/mcp_http.rs:845
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The last-commit fix only treats a cached session as stale when `session_is_stale()` sees `listener.is_closed()`, but that closed bit still depends on the `/mcp/sse` response stream having been dropped by the HTTP transport. `handle_mcp_sse()` does not synchronously invalidate the session on disconnect; it still returns an `Sse` stream and relies on channel teardown to happen later. That means an ordinary network-blip reconnect can still reach `get_or_create()` before hyper/axum has dropped the original stream, at which point `session_is_stale()` returns false and the reconnect reuses the old `McpServer` with its prior subscriptions. The new regression test does not cover this transport ordering because it calls `sessions.connect()` directly and drops the raw receiver, which closes the channel immediately instead of exercising actual `/mcp/sse` disconnect semantics.
Suggested fix: Tie HTTP-session invalidation to real SSE connection teardown rather than the eventual channel close signal, or introduce explicit server-issued session generations/tokens so a reconnect cannot silently attach to a logically closed session. Add a regression test that drives the live `/mcp/sse` transport, disconnects the client, reconnects immediately, and proves the new connection does not inherit prior subscriptions without a fresh `resources/subscribe` call.

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:689
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 0a54ba3d2cf27d69f74eb47909ce3777376193fb is the first commit that appends a closed hx-06abf84a row to .ddx/beads.jsonl, but that new row records both closing_commit_sha and the top-level <report-summary><commit> as fe8cc26b1ab73781316c54b1acbcfb80ea07e782, the prior implementation commit that fixed hx-7607eb91 rather than the execution that actually measured and closed hx-06abf84a itself. Evidence: git show fe8cc26b1ab73781316c54b1acbcfb80ea07e782:.ddx/beads.jsonl contains no hx-06abf84a row, while git show 0a54ba3d2cf27d69f74eb47909ce3777376193fb:.ddx/beads.jsonl shows the first closed row and it still names fe8cc26b1ab73781316c54b1acbcfb80ea07e782 as both closure fields. As a result, tracker queries against the latest closed hx-06abf84a record misattribute which commit actually measured and closed the governing bead, breaking the durable closure provenance required by HELIX report semantics.
Suggested fix: Append a new closed hx-06abf84a row that preserves the current PASS measure-results block and artifact .ddx/beads.jsonl:hx-7607eb91 but updates both closing_commit_sha and the top-level <report-summary><commit> to 0a54ba3d2cf27d69f74eb47909ce3777376193fb, with summary text that names that same close step.

acceptance: With AXON_NO_AUTH unset and an unreachable AXON_TAILSCALE_SOCKET, `axon-server --storage memory` fails startup instead of serving in anonymous-admin mode; with `--no-auth` or `AXON_NO_AUTH=1`, the same command starts successfully and logs the development bypass.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-server/src/main.rs:54
Category: security
Severity: high
Description: The new auth implementation still configures `no_auth` with `default_value = "true"`, so a plain `axon-server` boot enters the development bypass instead of enabling Tailscale identity resolution. Confirmed by running `RUST_LOG=info timeout 2 target/debug/axon-server --storage memory --http-port 0 --grpc-port 0`, which logs `running in --no-auth mode: all requests succeed as admin (actor=anonymous)` before serving both transports. ADR-005 and FEAT-012 define `--no-auth` as an explicit development escape hatch; leaving it on by default means the shipped server remains unauthenticated unless operators know to force auth on, which defeats the purpose of this implementation bead and leaves production deployments exposed to anonymous admin access.
Suggested fix: Make authenticated mode the default by removing the implicit no-auth default, so auth initialization runs unless `--no-auth` or `AXON_NO_AUTH=1` is explicitly provided. Add a startup regression test or CLI-level assertion that default startup requires auth initialization while the explicit bypass still works.
title: security: axon-server defaults to anonymous-admin mode

acceptance: Template PUT, GET, and DELETE succeed for registered qualified collection ids in non-default namespaces; qualified collections continue to render markdown correctly; tests cover at least one namespace-scoped template CRUD round trip.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:330
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:1509
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:1550
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:1568
File: crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs:606
File: crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs:1466
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:1263
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:7249
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new template CRUD methods gate on ensure_collection_exists, which checks the requested identifier against storage.list_collections(). Every storage adapter returns only bare collection names there, while collection views and markdown rendering already support qualified identifiers like prod.billing.tasks. As a result, a registered non-default collection can successfully render markdown through the existing qualified path, but the new public template PUT/GET/DELETE surface returns not found for that same qualified collection id. This breaks integration with Axon's namespace-aware collection model.
Suggested fix: Replace the bare-name existence check with namespace-aware resolution such as collection_registered_in_namespace or resolve_collection_key, and add handler or gateway coverage for template CRUD on a registered qualified collection identifier.
title: integration: template CRUD rejects qualified collection identifiers

acceptance: scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py .ddx/beads.jsonl --id does-not-exist exits non-zero and reports the missing bead ID, while scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py .ddx/beads.jsonl --id hx-f058cf32 --id hx-1012e833 still exits 0 after validating those existing records.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py:34
File: scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py:70
File: scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py:90
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new --id filter path can report a clean pass even when it validates nothing. validate_tracker() skips every record whose bead_id is not in included_ids, but it never records whether any requested IDs were actually seen before returning success when failures is empty. Reproduced on the reviewed commit with: scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py .ddx/beads.jsonl --id does-not-exist. The command exits 0 and prints '.ddx/beads.jsonl: all measure timestamps are <= updated_at' even though no bead was checked. Because this commit's durable gate example relies on scoped --id validation, that false-positive path weakens the audit value of the new validator and can let a typo or stale bead ID silently mark verification as passing.
Suggested fix: Track the requested IDs that were encountered during validation and return a non-zero error when any requested bead ID is missing, with stderr output naming the unmatched IDs. Keep the current success path for full-file validation and for scoped runs where every requested bead is actually checked.

acceptance: Rollback-related docs and planning artifacts use the same audit operation name(s) that the implementation emits for shipped rollback behavior; FEAT-023, FEAT-003, and rollback acceptance criteria agree on the chosen taxonomy for entity-level and future rollback events
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-023-rollback-recovery.md:44-45
File: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-003-audit-log.md:37
File: crates/axon-audit/src/entry.rs:44
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:1277-1294
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: FEAT-023 states that rollback creates audit entries with operation: rollback, and active rollback planning beads further encode names such as point_in_time_rollback. The shipped entity-level revert path does not emit those names: it records MutationType::EntityRevert, which serializes as entity.revert, and FEAT-003 now frames the audit contract as a dot-namespaced mutation taxonomy. That leaves the rollback feature spec, planning backlog, and current implementation speaking different audit operation names for the same behavior, which will break deterministic acceptance checks and confuse downstream consumers that filter audit entries by operation string.
Suggested fix: Choose and document one canonical rollback operation namespace, then align FEAT-023, related planning beads, and the implementation with that taxonomy. If entity-level rollback stays as entity.revert, say so explicitly in FEAT-023 and reserve distinct dot-namespaced values for future collection- or transaction-level rollback events.

acceptance: The Postgres-backed axon-storage tests provision their database from the test harness itself, and automated verification of those tests no longer requires .github/workflows/ci.yml to define a postgres service or AXON_TEST_POSTGRES job env for the tests to run.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:535
Related file: .github/workflows/ci.yml:23
Category: drift (rust-cargo)
Severity: medium
Description: The new Postgres regression coverage is enforced only by GitHub Actions job wiring: the test job provisions a postgres service and injects AXON_TEST_POSTGRES in .github/workflows/ci.yml, while the crate-side test code panics whenever CI is set but that workflow-specific environment is absent. I verified the coupling locally with CI=1 cargo test -p axon-storage postgres_roundtrip_when_available, which now fails immediately at crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:539. Active rust-cargo practices require tests needing real external services to use testcontainers, but this implementation keeps the backend provisioning outside the crate and tied to one CI workflow. That makes the regression test non-portable across automated environments and leaves the test contract implicit in workflow configuration rather than the test harness.
Suggested fix: Move the Postgres-backed tests to a self-contained harness, preferably testcontainers or an equivalent crate-owned provisioner, so the axon-storage tests bring up their own database during automated verification instead of depending on workflow-only services and env wiring.
title: drift: Postgres verification depends on workflow-specific service wiring

acceptance: Either: (1) axon.proto and service.rs implement markdown format support for GetEntity and contract tests cover rendered_markdown, while the documented HTTP path matches the gateway; or (2) FEAT-026 explicitly marks gRPC markdown rendering as deferred and documents the actual shipped HTTP route
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-markdown-templates.md:137-183
File: crates/axon-server/proto/axon.proto:17-24
File: crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:157-173
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The newly added FEAT-026 spec presents the V1 markdown GET surface as both `GET /v1/collections/{collection}/entities/{id}?format=markdown` and a gRPC `GetEntity` contract with a `format` field plus `rendered_markdown` in the response. The reviewed implementation only ships an unversioned HTTP route (`/collections/{collection}/entities/{id}`) and leaves the protobuf/service path JSON-only. Because gRPC is not listed under deferred surfaces, the feature spec currently overstates what this commit actually made available.
Suggested fix: Align the FEAT-026 API surface with reality by either implementing the documented gRPC/request-response additions and matching contract tests, or by marking markdown rendering as HTTP-only in this phase and moving gRPC support into the deferred-surfaces section. Also correct the documented HTTP path if `/v1` is not part of the current gateway contract.
title: correctness: FEAT-026 API surface overstates shipped markdown GET support

acceptance: put_collection_view returns an error when the target collection is not registered. SQLite and Postgres schema constrain collection_views to existing collections. Conformance tests cover the unregistered-collection case across adapters.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs:70
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new collection_views storage is not constrained to registered collections. The SQLite and Postgres DDL create collection_views without a foreign key to collections, and the in-memory adapter's put_collection_view path also writes without checking that the collection is registered. That allows orphan views to be persisted for never-created collections, which breaks the feature spec's collection association invariant and makes cleanup bugs harder to prevent.
Suggested fix: Enforce collection existence before storing a view in every adapter, add SQL foreign keys where applicable, and add conformance tests asserting put_collection_view fails for an unregistered collection.
title: correctness: collection views can be stored for collections that do not exist
